name: TAO Delayed Retest Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: breakout continuation with delayed entry after retest rather than immediate trigger
thesis: TAO 4h is the best structurally different lane for testing whether breakout continuation is more robust when entered on the delayed retest instead of the first impulse bar. In a mixed chop/transition backdrop, delayed retests should filter part of the false-break problem while preserving enough expansion to matter.
invalidation_condition: Abort if retest entries are too sparse, if retests fail and never continue, or if TAO noise overwhelms the delayed-entry advantage.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price breaks from local balance, retests the breakout zone or fast value, holds, and then continues in the breakout direction on the delayed entry.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Buying or selling the retest after a breakout should avoid some false first-bar entries while still participating when the move is real.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h should express breakout/retest structure more visibly than ETH while remaining tradable at a swing timeframe.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Breakouts may retest correctly but still fail to continue enough after fees, especially if the regime remains too mixed.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If retests occur with acceptable density but continuation is weak, tighten the continuation qualifier before narrowing the retest window.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A healthy number of retest entries with better quality than first-bar breakout chasing and a mix of first-target wins plus occasional runners.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h and at least one cleaner lane show delayed-retest continuation with credible density and positive post-cost movement.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too-sparse retests, or repeated retest failures that reverse immediately.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; breakout, retest, and directional handoff are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: broader than exact breakout-candle triggers because retest windows allow natural continuation structure

validation_basket.why_included: Best lane in the basket for visibly testing delayed retest continuation.
validation_basket.continue_if: Retest entries are frequent enough and continue after costs.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Retests are sparse or fail immediately.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Breakout, orderly retest, then a second-leg continuation push.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same delayed-retest idea survives on a cleaner liquid lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC preserves retest continuation behavior with acceptable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC becomes too muted for controlled retest entries.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Cleaner but less explosive retest continuations than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the mechanism transfers to the cleaner major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH still shows the retest-and-go structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The mechanism only works on high-beta lanes.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner delayed retest continuations than TAO.

long: Scale out remaining 25% on ADX_14 rollover or 5 bars
short: Scale out remaining 25% on ADX_14 rollover or 5 bars
